title: Ex-PM Attal is officially running for president and wants to distance himself from Macron
subhead: Gabriel Attal announced his presidential bid on Friday. He's hoping to quickly build momentum in order to overtake Edouard Philippe, another former prime minister who is tipped as the favorite to represent the centrist bloc and the right.

title: Elysée Palace searched as part of investigation into events contracts
Iran is considering taxing users of undersea cables in the Strait of Hormuz
Coordinator of 2015 Paris attacks could be released from prison early under Belgian law
subhead: Sentenced in France for his role in the terrorist attacks that killed 132 people on November 13, 2015, Mohamed Bakkali was, in theory, not eligible for release under French law before 2035. But in Belgium, where he is incarcerated, he benefits from a significantly more lenient sentencing regime.

title: Ebola in the DRC: 'The epidemic is unprecedented in scale'
subhead: While it is unlikely that the disease will spread globally, there is a 'high' risk that it will continue to gain ground in Central Africa, the World Health Organization has warned.

title: Israel has damaged or destroyed 45% of towns in southern Lebanon
subhead: According to an exclusive analysis of radar satellite images conducted by Le Monde and Masae Analytics, nearly half of the urban centers in southern Lebanon have been affected or even destroyed since the beginning of Israel's ground offensive in March 2026.

title: Canal+ 'blacklist': Over half of recent French films credit people now threatened with retribution
subhead: The CEO of Canal+ has threatened to boycott film industry figures who signed an open letter against the company's owner, Vincent Bolloré. Together, the signatories are credited in over 600 films produced in France over the past five years.

War in Ukraine: Kherson and Oleshky, two different worlds just 10 kilometers apart
subhead: On the front line, the small town of Oleshky in the south has been occupied by the Russian military since the invasion began. Residents try to reach Kherson at great personal risk.
byline: Thomas d’Istria and  Virginie NGuyen Hoang

title: Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
subhead: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

title: From Italy to Libya, the dehumanizing processing of migrants
subhead: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (4/4). Exploited, extorted and abused in Libya, exiles who survive the crossing are rendered invisible and sometimes fall into the hands of mafias linked to agricultural businesses. But 'what matters is that irregular arrivals decrease,' as an official in Rome put it.
